test.command: PYTEST_ADDOPTS='-p no:pytest_ethereum' BROWNIE_NETWORK=mainnet .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_misc_fixes.py tests/test_constants.py tests/test_time.py -W ignore -s --tb=short -v
test.notes: 15 passed, 2 failed. Failures: test_stablecoins[0xf939E0A03FB07F59A73314E73794Be0E57ac1b4E] (crvUSD returns $0.9996 not $1) and test_stablecoins[0x853d955aCEf822Db058eb8505911ED77F175b99e] (FRAX returns $0.9987 not $1). Root cause: the ypricemagic DB bucket cache (~/.ypricemagic/ypricemagic.sqlite) has these tokens cached as 'chainlink feed' bucket from previous test runs. check_bucket() in y/prices/utils/buckets.py reads DB cache first and returns without checking string_matchers (STABLECOINS). Separate run of 61 non-stablecoin baseline tests all passed.

typecheck.notes: mypy crashes internally with AssertionError on mypyc-compiled modules (Cannot find component EnvironmentVariable_6e898407ec). This is a pre-existing issue with the mypyc compiled environment, consistent with prior milestone scrutiny findings. The crash produces no useful type error output but is not caused by the misc-bug-fixes changes. Treating as pre-existing.

lint.command: N/A - ruff and flake8 not installed in project venv
lint.notes: No linter installed in .venv. Code follows existing project conventions (same as prior milestones).

blockingIssues.description: test_constants.py fails for crvUSD and FRAX. When these tokens are added to STABLECOINS in y/constants.py, check_bucket() in y/prices/utils/buckets.py still returns 'chainlink feed' for them because the ypricemagic DB bucket cache has cached entries from previous test runs that predate the STABLECOINS addition. check_bucket() reads DB first and returns immediately without consulting string_matchers. The fix is to check string_matchers BEFORE the DB cache (since string_matchers are pure functions requiring no I/O), ensuring tokens in STABLECOINS are always classified correctly regardless of DB state.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: In check_bucket() (y/prices/utils/buckets.py), move the string_matchers loop BEFORE the db.get_bucket() call. Since string_matchers are pure synchronous functions (lambda comparisons against STABLECOINS and other constant sets), they should take precedence over any cached DB entry. This also eliminates the DB cache invalidation problem.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: When adding tokens to STABLECOINS or other string_matchers in y/constants.py, be aware that existing DB bucket cache entries at ~/.ypricemagic/ypricemagic.sqlite may override the new classification. The check_bucket() function reads DB first. Workers adding new stablecoins should verify that the test_constants.py tests pass, and if they fail for new stablecoins, the root cause is likely stale DB cache entries.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: misc-bug-fixes feature added FRAX and crvUSD to STABLECOINS, but test_constants.py failed for both because DB had them cached as 'chainlink feed'. The DB query confirmed: sqlite3 ~/.ypricemagic/ypricemagic.sqlite showed bucket='chainlink feed' for both tokens.
